question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordItem with a forged SignatureMap (empty or mismatched sigPairs) that passes through doParse() without any signature validation, causing recordItemListener.onItem(recordItem) to persist an unauthorized transaction as if it were legitimately signed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged user inject a RecordItem whose consensusTimestamp falls within the dateRangeFilter window but whose SignedTransaction contains no valid signer signatures, causing the item to be forwarded to recordItemListener.onItem() and persisted as an authorized transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.accept()] Can an unprivileged user craft a RecordItem with nonce=0 in the TransactionID so that isTopLevel() returns true unconditionally, bypassing any parent-authorization check and causing the aggregator to treat an unsigned child transaction as a top-level authorized transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.accept()] Can an unprivileged user craft a RecordItem with nonce>0 and scheduled=true in the TransactionID so that isTopLevel() returns true, causing a scheduled transaction to be treated as owner-approved even when the required signer threshold has not been met, leading to unauthorized execution being recorded?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged user manipulate the consensusTimestamp of a RecordItem to fall exactly on the boundary of dateRangeFilter (timestamp >= start && timestamp <= end) so that a transaction with no valid owner signatures passes the filter and is forwarded to recordItemListener.onItem(), resulting in unauthorized persistence?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged user trigger the @Retryable mechanism by causing a transient failure during doParse(), so that a partially-processed RecordFile containing unsigned transactions is retried and eventually committed, bypassing any one-time authorization check that was expected to gate the transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the @Transactional boundary on parse() so that a RecordFile containing transactions with forged or absent owner signatures is committed atomically to the database before any downstream authorization check can reject it, leading to unauthorized transactions being permanently stored?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged user supply a RecordFile whose items list contains a RecordItem with an empty SignatureMap (no sigPairs), and since doParse() never inspects the SignatureMap before calling recordItemListener.onItem(), cause the transaction to be persisted as if it had valid owner authorization?
